name: Incirlik Air Base
tagline: A strategic U.S. and NATO air base in southern Turkey, currently a restricted, unaccompanied tour.
_note: True OCONUS (Turkey). 39th Air Base Wing; strategic US/NATO base near Adana. RESTRICTED/UNACCOMPANIED: command-sponsored dependents were ordered to depart in 2016 and the tour has been unaccompanied/restricted since -> unaccompanied pattern: no family housing/OHA/DoDEA/BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy framed as normal. ACCURACY NOTE: accompanied status could change; guide tells users to confirm current status with the gaining command. Personnel live in on-base quarters. Facts verified from official sources (incirlik.af.mil, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx); current accompanied-status is policy-dependent and may change.

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (on a Turkish/NATO base)
data.Role: Strategic U.S. and NATO air operations and logistics
data.Note: Command-sponsored dependents were ordered to depart in 2016

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Incirlik Air Base, home of the 39th Air Base Wing, is a strategic U.S. and NATO air base in southern Turkey near Adana, long important for regional operations and logistics.

Since 2016, command-sponsored dependents have not been authorized, making it a restricted, unaccompanied tour. Policy can change, so confirm the current accompanied status with your gaining command before you plan.

data.subtitle: As of now, Incirlik is a restricted, unaccompanied tour: families do not relocate here, and personnel live on base. The most important step is confirming the current status, which is policy-dependent.
intro: Command-sponsored dependents were ordered to depart in 2016, and the tour has been unaccompanied/restricted since. So there's no family housing, OHA, BAH, or DoDEA school in the usual sense.
Because this status is policy-dependent and could change, the first thing to do is confirm the current accompanied status and any movement restrictions with your gaining command.

recommendations.advice: Accompanied status at Incirlik is policy-driven; verify with your gaining command before making any family plans.
Personnel live in on-base quarters; plan for an unaccompanied tour.
Sort out where your family lives during the tour and your finances and communication plan early.
Unaccompanied/restricted tours have their own pay and allowance rules; verify everything with finance.

sections.title: Access
data: Incirlik is a Turkish/NATO base hosting U.S. forces, with a heightened security posture and access controlled by both U.S. and host-nation rules.
Requirements and any movement restrictions change with conditions, so coordinate everything through your command before you travel.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base Quarters)
data: As an unaccompanied/restricted tour, personnel live in on-base quarters; there is no command-sponsored family housing.
Your unit handles your quarters assignment on arrival; confirm current arrangements and any policy changes through your sponsor and the base.

sections.title: Schools
data: Because Incirlik is currently a restricted, unaccompanied tour, dependents do not relocate here. (A DoDEA school operated here when the base was accompanied; confirm the current status with your command.)
If you have school-age children, they remain at your designated location during the tour; coordinate schooling there.

data.Care: Primary and urgent care for the assigned population; complex cases are referred/evacuated (e.g., to Landstuhl)

intro: A restricted, unaccompanied tour has its own checklist. Confirm the current status first, then focus on on-base in-processing and the home front.

steps.title: Confirm current accompanied status
steps.text: Incirlik's status is policy-dependent; verify with your gaining command before making any family plans.

steps.title: Coordinate travel with your command
steps.text: Travel and reporting are tightly coordinated under a heightened security posture; confirm your itinerary and entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get the required ID and logistics cards.

steps.title: Get your quarters set up
steps.text: Coordinate your on-base quarters with your unit; this is your home for the tour.

steps.title: Square away the home front
steps.text: Confirm where your family lives during the tour, sort finances, allotments, powers of attorney, and a communication plan.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program; complex care is referred or evacuated, so understand the process.

data.q: Can my family come to Incirlik?
data.a: Currently no. Command-sponsored dependents were ordered to depart in 2016 and the tour has been unaccompanied/restricted since. This is policy-dependent, so confirm the current status with your gaining command.

data.q: Do I get BAH or OHA?
data.a: As an unaccompanied/restricted tour, the usual family housing allowances don't apply the same way. Confirm your specific pay and allowances (and any continued BAH for family back home) with finance.
In on-base quarters. The unit handles your assignment when you arrive.
Yes. Accompanied status is policy-driven and has changed before, so always verify the current situation with your command.

sections.title: On-Base & Around
sections.note: Off-base movement may be restricted by the security posture; this is area context, not travel guidance.

picks.name: Base MWR & community
picks.blurb: On-base recreation and morale programs anchor the tour.

picks.name: Adana
picks.blurb: The nearby southern Turkish city (access subject to force-protection rules).

picks.name: Mediterranean coast
picks.blurb: Turkey's southern Mediterranean shoreline lies to the south.

picks.name: Regional history
picks.blurb: Southern Turkey is rich in ancient sites (access permitting).
